matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Rashi on I Chronicles 7:1:1
matches.x: And of the sons of Issachar: Tola, and Puah, and Jashub, and Shimron In the Pentateuch (Gen. 46:13) it is written: “and Job.” Job was his name [originally], but since they settled themselves (נִתְיַשְּׁבוּ) to learn Torah, as it is written (below 12:33): “And of the sons of Issachar, who possessed understanding of the times,” he merited and was called Jashub (יָשוּב).
The sons of Issachar: Tola, Puva, Yov, and Shimron.

“And these are the names of the children of Israel who were coming to Egypt, Jacob and his sons: The firstborn of Jacob, Reuben” (Genesis 46:8). “And the sons of Issachar: Tola, and Puva, and Yov, and Shimron” (Genesis 46:13). “And these are the names of the children of Israel who were coming to Egypt…. And the sons of Issachar: Tola, and Puva, and Yov, and Shimron” – Rabbi Meir saw a certain…

25 bare unto him, Manasseh, and Ephraim—three. And the sons of Benjamin: Bela and Becher,

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Radak on I Chronicles 8:1:1
matches.x: Benjamin begot Bela his first-born, Ashbel the second. The text says “Ashbel the second” when he was really the third; for the sons of Benjamin were Bela, Becher, and Ashbel (Gen 46:21). Indeed, above it does say “Bela, Becher” (7:6). But because it does not mention Becher [here], it says “Ashbel the second.” It does not mention Becher here much as it does not mention him in parashat…

“And the sons of Benjamin: Bela, and Bekher, and Ashbel, Gera, and Naaman, Eḥi, and Rosh, Mupim, and Ḥupim, and Ard” (Genesis 46:21). “And the sons of Benjamin: Bela…” – when Joseph stood with Benjamin, he asked him: ‘Do you have sons?’ He said to him: ‘Yes.’ He said to him: ‘How many?’ He said to him: ‘Ten.’ He said: ‘What are their names?’ He said to him: ‘I had one brother. His actions were…
The sons of Binyomin [were]: Bela, Becher, Ashbeil, Geiroh, Naamon, Eichi, Rosh, Muppim, Chuppim and Ard.

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Rashi on I Chronicles 7:6:1
matches.x: Benjamin: Bela, and Becher, and Jediael This is the one called Ashbel in the “Book of the Just.” (Gen. 46:21).

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Radak on I Chronicles 7:8:1
matches.x: The sons of Becher … Anathoth. The city of Anathoth that is in the land of Benjamin is named for him, because this Anathoth built it. This is similar to: “and he named the city for his son Enoch” (Gen 4:17).

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Radak on I Chronicles 7:21:1
matches.x: The men of Gath killed them, the ones born in the land. Some explain (Pseudo-Rashi) that the reason the text says “the ones born in the land” is that the men of Gath were born in the land, and knew the lay of the land; so when the Ephraimites came into the land to take their cattle and did not know the roads, the men of Gath ambushed them and killed them. And some (?) explain that it says “the…
